item.title: Long spoiler post ahead
item.content:encoded:
So this is one of thoses old school puzzle SCPs I used to love reading. I'm happy with the story focused stuff modern writers like to write, but this brings back a primal joy of having to take a deeper read at some of the stuff .

Spoilers below

+ show block
– hide block

So theres hidden code in the page source, you get a hint of this where Footnote 1 doesn't seem to exist anywhere in the article. When you do open it, you get glimpses of a skip from another world.

We learn that something messed up, something got switched, something messy is going on. Entering the location of the good doctor's office leads to the forest. Lets call our reality Reality A and their dimension Reality B

There appears to be two researchers missing, one of them Senior Researcher Daniel Schröder, the other Senior Researcher ████ ███ [DATA CORRUPT]. SResearcher Blackbox's name is definitely shorter than Daniel's, having only 5 letters in his first name. We know some realities definitely got their wires crossed because the placard attached to the office door of Reality A belongs to Dr. Blackbox of Reality B, along with the fact that the power of Reality A crossed over to Reality B, causing a power failure to one and a power overload to the other.

Interestingly enough, it appears that the anomaly Reality B is dealing with is not a forest, but a "featureless horizontal plane composed of an undestructible white substance". I'm not entirely sure yet what this is, it could be Dr. Daniel's desk for all I know, and the sun overhead could be a ceiling light, but its interesting enough to note of the stark difference between our forest of dead skin, and their desert of white.

And now things get a bit interesting, as the report starts describing the office itself. The atmosphere of the Forest can be changed by a thermostat (?) probably, because it changes the vents in Dr. Daniel's office. Unconfirmed if the generator is sustaining the anomalous world's atmosphere through those vents but its the leading theory. Doing a bit of googling, the Swiss Pine/ Pinus cembra is a species thats often used in Bonsais and carvings. Dr. Daniel probably had one on his desk and it managed to integrate into this magical dead skin world. Thats three features of his office that turned into part of the landscape. Due to the fact that this office is located on the moon, there was very little introduced biomatter, with maybe a few specks of grass or dirt that managed to sneak in, producing the rest of the endemic grass species.

The log is uninteresting, no cognitohazards or probability hazards happened. I'd upvote for the simple reason of not having yet another MTF slaughterfest because that seems to be too creative an idea for this wiki.

And now we get to the twist of this article, which I'm going to have to do multiple takes to fully understand. The beautiful scenic forest is actually literal piss and shit. First, the DNA isn't the one of Dr. Daniel, but the one of Dr. Blackbox. If I'm understanding right, theres a placard of Reality B on the office of Reality A leading to an anomaly on the DNA of Reality B's doctor.

You know theres strong evidence for a theory here that the Reality A's anomaly is literally just a piece of poop floating in a puddle of piss in a toilet, and the Reality B's anomaly is the toilet seat (a white flat expanse, etc). I hope to god this is the case.

This next part, I believe is the most important, but I can't figure it out yet. It deals a lot with temporal shenanigans. 9 years later, the Foundation decides to explore 4379 again, and finds a building thats been inhabited before, and contains writings from the future. I can't make heads or tails of it so I'll put it in bullet points for people in the comment section to try to take a crack at it:

  • Theres a man in black clothing trying to leave 4379. Because of temporal shenanigans, it might be coming now, tomorrow, yesterday, whenever.
  • Reality B on its side, sees an entity traveling temporally weird from within 4379 to the moon. I personally believe this means that theyre observing Dr. Daniel moving from within the teleporting kiddie pool to his office on the moon. Because the kiddie pool bends the laws of space, it should also bend the laws of time, and we're seeing all the side effects.
  • Something something something took a spacesuit and went to Earth a year after they found the weird house
  • Fecal matter comes from digested Swiss pine seeds, the building is devoted to storing swiss pine seeds.
  • Whatever happened to Dr. Daniel? We know we're standing in Dr. Blackbox's DNA, but so far Dr. Daniel doesnt appear in Reality B's anomaly (unless theyre standing on bone or enamel)

Had fun with this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+
-

Looking at the page source reveals quite a bit of hidden text I hadn't seen. I feel like this article goes even deeper than I first realized now, and I'm really intrigued and captivated. Great job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Intentional; one has to (intentionally) hide the text the footnote appears in for that to happen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a really interesting read! I like the twist, and the dialogue between the 3 MTF agents was very well-written! +1

+ Possible Spoiler?
- Possible Spoiler?

There's a footnote at the bottom of the page that doesn't actually show up anywhere in the article. Is that intentional or just an artifact from the writing process?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: an MTF log that doesn't kill everybody but still feels realistic? Hell yeah!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
seconding the people who liked it better without the final collapsible. Not everything needs to be ultra spooky-ooky and I think this was stronger as a character study of three ordinary (and in one case very paranoid) agents exploring a mundane anomalous space.

No vote for the time being.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it. Strange, eerie, implications but not proof.

It says nowhere that the skips dna is the missing person, I'm going with that the individual never existed and all suggestions to the contrary are a function of the skip making you doubt your own memory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I rather liked the Exploration Log, it was great at showing just how bored (and, as a result, kinda jumpy) these guys were. The really uneventful nature of the log only reinforced it.

I felt the double twist at the end kind of… mixed. The first twist was quite interesting, because it only raised more questions about what, exactly, had happened. The second twist felt much less powerful, largely because it felt almost like it was trying to act against the first (and, in my opinion, better) twist.

+ show block
– hide block

There is, however, one question left unanswered following the end of the article1: If everything is made from the internal fluids of the missing director, where'd the trees and grass come from?!

Footnotes
1. You don't need to answer it, I just wanted it pointed out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this could stand on its own without the twist, personally. That isn't to say I like it less with the twist, just that I feel it is not strictly necessary. Either way, have a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really, really like this one, but now I'm devastatingly curious about the - A.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the best reads since last month. Excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this until the note at the end. Personally I just think it’s unnecessary body horror.

Big thanks to riyangendut for telling me helping me how to grow a forest on the moon, and to everyone who took a look and gave feedback as a draft.

More to come.
